heading: 9.7.16.9.1.1.Decompression of 4-bit floating point to 8-bit type
content:
A contiguous set of 16 elements of 4-bits each followed by 8 bytes of padding can be converted
into 16 elements of 8-bits each as shown in [Figure 194](#tcgen05-decompression-4b8b).



[Figure: tcgen05-decompression-4b8b]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:
This figure illustrates the decompression operation that occurs when copying data from Shared Memory to Tensor Memory using the tcgen05.cp instruction. Specifically, it shows how 4-bit packed data (.b4x16_p64 format) stored in Shared Memory is decompressed/expanded into 8-bit data (.b8x16 format) in Tensor Memory. Each 4-bit element is unpacked into an 8-bit element, effectively doubling the number of bytes used to represent the same data.

COMPONENTS AND LAYOUT:

Top Section - Shared Memory:
- Label: "Shared Memory" appears at the top left
- Format label: ".b4x16_p64" appears above the right-center portion of the memory bar
- Represented as a single horizontal bar containing many small cells (columns)
- The bar is narrower/shorter in total visual width compared to the Tensor Memory bar below, reflecting the packed/compressed nature of the 4-bit data
- The cells are colored in a repeating pattern of colors: green, white/light, blue/light blue, yellow/orange, peach/salmon, green, white, yellow, and other pastel shades
- The colored region occupies roughly the left half to two-thirds of the bar, with the right portion appearing mostly white/empty (uncolored cells)
- Black vertical dividers separate individual cells
- The colored cells represent the actual 4-bit packed data elements

Arrow (Data Flow):
- A solid blue downward-pointing arrow is positioned in the center between the two memory bars
- Label: "tcgen05.cp" appears to the right of the arrow
- This indicates the PTX instruction that performs the copy and decompression operation

Bottom Section - Tensor Memory:
- Label: "Tensor Memory" appears at the bottom left
- Format label: ".b8x16" appears below the center of the memory bar
- Represented as a single horizontal bar that is visually wider/longer than the Shared Memory bar above
- The bar contains more cells than the Shared Memory bar, and ALL cells across the entire bar are colored (no white/empty cells visible)
- The same color pattern is present but now spread across the full width of the bar: green, blue/light blue, yellow/orange, peach/salmon, green, blue, yellow, peach, and repeating pastel colors
- The colors correspond to the same data elements as in Shared Memory but each element now occupies a wider (8-bit) cell
- Black vertical dividers separate individual cells

NUMERICAL VALUES AND DIMENSIONS:
- Source format: .b4x16_p64 — 4-bit elements, 16 elements per group, packed with 64-bit packing
- Destination format: .b8x16 — 8-bit elements, 16 elements per group
- The decompression ratio is 2:1 in terms of bits per element (4-bit to 8-bit), meaning the data expands to twice the storage size
- The Tensor Memory bar visually appears approximately twice as wide as the populated (colored) portion of the Shared Memory bar, consistent with the 2x expansion

DATA FLOW AND ORGANIZATION PATTERN:
- Data flows from top (Shared Memory) to bottom (Tensor Memory) via the tcgen05.cp instruction
- Each 4-bit element in the packed Shared Memory representation is decompressed into a corresponding 8-bit element in Tensor Memory
- The color coding is consistent between source and destination: matching colors in both bars represent the same logical data elements, demonstrating that the values are preserved but stored in a wider format
- The packed source (.b4x16_p64) has elements stored compactly; the destination (.b8x16) has each element zero-extended or sign-extended to 8 bits
- The overall data pattern (color sequence) is maintained across the decompression, confirming element-to-element correspondence

TEXT LABELS SUMMARY:
- "Shared Memory" — identifies the source memory type
- ".b4x16_p64" — identifies the source data format (4-bit elements, 16 per tile, 64-bit packed)
- "tcgen05.cp" — the PTX instruction performing the decompression copy
- "Tensor Memory" — identifies the destination memory type
- ".b8x16" — identifies the destination data format (8-bit elements, 16 per tile)


Figure 194 Decompression from 4-bit to 8-bit

The individual 4-bit to 8-bit decompression would look like as shown in [Figure 195](#tcgen05-decompression-4b8b-individual).



[Figure: tcgen05-decompression-4b8b-individual]
Individual Decompression from 4-bit to 8-bit

CONCEPT:
This figure illustrates how a single 4-bit floating point value is decompressed/expanded into an 8-bit floating point value. It shows the bit-field mapping between a compact 4-bit representation and its expanded 8-bit form.

TOP ROW - 8-bit Source Register (Input):
A row of 8 individual bit cells numbered from right to left: 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1, 0
These represent bit positions 0 through 7 of an 8-bit value.
All cells are shown as plain white/empty boxes.

MIDDLE SECTION - 4-bit Field Extraction:
Three labeled fields are extracted from specific bit positions of the 8-bit input:
- S (Sign bit): shown in green/light green background, occupies 1 bit
- E2 (Exponent field): shown in yellow/light yellow background, occupies 2 bits
- M1 (Mantissa field): shown in orange/light orange background, occupies 1 bit

These three fields together form the 4-bit compressed representation (1 + 2 + 1 = 4 bits total).

ARROW:
A large solid blue downward-pointing arrow indicates the decompression transformation direction, from the 4-bit extracted fields to the 8-bit output.

BOTTOM ROW - 8-bit Output (Decompressed Result):
A row of 8 bit cells representing the expanded 8-bit value, laid out from left to right:
- Bit 7: 0 (plain white, zero-filled)
- Bit 6: 0 (plain white, zero-filled)
- Bit 5: S (green background, sign bit placed here)
- Bit 4-3: E2 (yellow background, exponent bits placed here)
- Bit 2: M1 (orange background, mantissa bit placed here)
- Bit 1: 0 (plain white, zero-filled)
- Bit 0: 0 (plain white, zero-filled)

DATA FLOW / MAPPING PATTERN:
The 4-bit fields (S, E2, M1) are extracted from the input 8-bit register and then repositioned within an 8-bit output word. The upper 2 bits (positions 7-6) are zero-filled, the lower 2 bits (positions 1-0) are zero-filled, and the S, E2, M1 fields are placed in the middle bit positions (5, 4-3, 2) of the 8-bit output. This represents a format conversion where the compact 4-bit float fields are mapped into corresponding fields of an 8-bit float format with zero padding on both ends.


Figure 195 Individual decompression from 4-bit to 8-bit
